source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/05_해외 등록 서류 (영업팀 F-up)/260213 태국 MD Healthcard Co., Ltd/251209 보완서류/2.2 [Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector] Technical Document/A-CSDT-23G attachment (Rev.5)/9. Validation and verification/9.2 Biological assessment/Biocompatibility Assessment Report.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Biocompatibility assessment report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports selection and assessment of biological evaluation tests according to FDA biocompatibility guideline and ISO 10993 standards.
keyPoints: The device contact category is skin surface with limited exposure of 24 hours or less.
Selected biological evaluation tests are cytotoxicity, sensitization, and irritation or intracutaneous reactivity.
Korea Testing Laboratory test results for cytotoxicity, irritation or intracutaneous reactivity, and sensitization are reported as satisfied.
The final assessment states the device is suitable to use as a medical device and biological safety based on the results.

entities.name: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW
entities.evidence: for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: FDA
entities.evidence: Selection of biological evaluation test according to FDA biocompatibility guideline

concepts.name: Biocompatibility assessment
concepts.evidence: The purpose of this assessment is to ensure that no undue risks arise from biological hazards associated with materials of manufacture and final devices.

concepts.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
concepts.evidence: Common name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

concepts.name: Skin surface limited exposure
concepts.evidence: Nature of body contact: Skin surface; Duration of contact: Limited exposure (≤ 24 Hours)

concepts.name: ISO 10993 biological evaluation
concepts.evidence: Use of International Standard ISO-10993, ‘Biological Evaluation of Medical Devices - Part 1: Evaluation and Testing

evidence.claim: The document is Biocompatibility Assessment Report A-TCF-5-144 Rev.0 dated Nov/10/2022.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-TCF-5-144 Rev. No. 0 Biocompatibility Assessment Report Rev. Date Nov/10/2022

evidence.claim: The assessed product models are A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Biocompatibility Assessment Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The device is a Flat Panel Digital X-ray Detector with wired Ethernet and wireless Wi-Fi communication.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is a Flat Panel Digital X-ray Detector... The device communicates by the wired Ethernet and wireless Wi-Fi communication feature.

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd.
The device is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems and support diagnoses by physicians and radiologists.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnoses for physicians and radiologists.

evidence.claim: The contact part is a deco sheet.
The biological contact categorization is skin surface with limited exposure of 24 hours or less.
evidence.evidenceText: Nature of body contact: Skin surface; Duration of contact: Limited exposure (≤ 24 Hours)

evidence.claim: The test selection follows FDA biocompatibility guideline using ISO 10993 biological evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: Selection of biological evaluation test according to FDA biocompatibility guideline (Use of International Standard ISO-10993, ‘Biological Evaluation of Medical Devices - Part 1: Evaluation and Testing)

evidence.claim: The selected biological evaluation tests are cytotoxicity, sensitization, and irritation or intracutaneous reactivity.
evidence.evidenceText: Tests selection Cytotoxicity O; Sensitization (Delayed-type hypersensitivity) O; Irritation or intracutaneous reactivity O

evidence.claim: Applied standards include ISO 10993-1, ISO 10993-5, ISO 10993-10, and ISO 10993-12.
evidence.evidenceText: Applied Standards - ISO 10993-1: Selection of Tests - ISO 10993-5: Test for Cytotoxicity - ISO 10993-10: Test for Irritation and Sensitization - ISO 10993-12: Sample Preparation

evidence.claim: Korea Testing Laboratory reported cytotoxicity testing to ISO 10993-5:2009 as satisfied.
evidence.evidenceText: Cytotoxicity ISO 10993-5:2009 Korea Testing Laboratory (KTL) 20-0094 Satisfied

evidence.claim: Korea Testing Laboratory reported irritation or intracutaneous reactivity testing to ISO 10993-10:2010 as satisfied.
evidence.evidenceText: Irritation or Intracutaneous Reactivity ISO 10993-10:2010 Korea Testing Laboratory (KTL) 20-0095 Satisfied

evidence.claim: Korea Testing Laboratory reported sensitization testing to ISO 10993-10:2010 as satisfied.
evidence.evidenceText: Sensitization ISO 10993-10:2010 Korea Testing Laboratory (KTL) 20-0096 Satisfied

evidence.claim: The final assessment concludes the product is suitable to use as a medical device and biological safety based on the results.
evidence.evidenceText: In conclusion, A1417MCW/A1717MCW/F1417MCW is suitable to use as medical device and biological safety based on the above results.

reviewFlags.reason: The source references FDA biocompatibility guideline, but the document itself appears to be manufacturer technical documentation rather than an FDA-issued document.
Appendix MSDS pages include substantial low-quality OCR or garbled text, especially the PET INK 9000 SERIES section.
The document contains appended MSDS materials with separate supplier dates and revisions; these were not treated as the main document date or revision.
